# path-migration-guard-allowlist.txt — E97-S5
#
# Allowlist for path-migration-guard.bats. Each line is one entry:
#   - format: <relative-path>[:<line-number>][:<pattern>]
#   - comments (#) and blank lines are ignored
#   - matches are exact-path-prefix on the file column; line/pattern columns
#     are optional further-narrowing (omit to allow all hits in the file)
#
# Why an entry belongs here:
#   1. Legitimate legacy fallback chain entry below a canonical .gaia/ check
#      (the executable code path that runs on pre-migration installs).
#   2. Documentation/comment intentionally describing the dual-layout contract
#      (e.g., ADR-111 cross-references, validator.md persona_sig docstring).
#   3. Retired scripts under scripts/retired/ (one-shot tools, intentionally
#      preserved; guarded against in-place resurrection by their tombstones).
#   4. Test fixtures under tests/fixtures/ and tests/**.bats that exercise
#      legacy-layout behavior (pre-migration regression coverage).
#   5. CHANGELOG.md historical entries (frozen — never rewritten).
#   6. Generated artifacts the guard would otherwise flag as drift.
#
# Adding a new entry requires PR review — keep the comment column explaining
# the rationale so the next reader (or auditor on a STALE_TARGET review) can
# evaluate whether the exception is still load-bearing.

# --- Bucket 1: legitimate legacy fallback chains (canonical .gaia/ first) ---

# scripts/lib/gaia-paths.sh exports the canonical constants; legacy refs are
# documented in docstrings only.
plugins/gaia/scripts/lib/gaia-paths.sh

# scripts/sprint-state.sh implements the smart-fallback resolve_paths()
# function that the entire framework relies on.
plugins/gaia/scripts/sprint-state.sh

# scripts/resolve-config.sh — E28-S9 / E28-S142 / E28-S191 path discovery
# precedence list documents both layouts as a contract.
plugins/gaia/scripts/resolve-config.sh

# scripts/validate-gate.sh — line 410 docstring documents the dual layout;
# lines 423/452/551 are executable cfg= legacy fallback assignments below
# the `if [ -f .gaia/config/...` canonical-first checks (E96-S1 / ADR-111).
plugins/gaia/scripts/validate-gate.sh

# scripts/config-hydration.sh — E97-S1 wires `.gaia/config/` first; legacy
# fallback at lines 387+389+395 retained.
plugins/gaia/scripts/lib/config-hydration.sh

# scripts/sprint-status-dashboard.sh — E97-S3 / E96-S7 partial-4b smart-fallback.
plugins/gaia/scripts/sprint-status-dashboard.sh

# scripts/check-status-discipline.sh — E97-S3 classify_path supports both layouts.
plugins/gaia/scripts/check-status-discipline.sh

# scripts/retro-sidecar-write.sh — NFR-RIM-2 allowlist intentionally covers both layouts.
plugins/gaia/scripts/retro-sidecar-write.sh

# scripts/statusline.sh — E96-S8 walk-up loop canonical-first.
plugins/gaia/scripts/statusline.sh

# scripts/review-summary-gen.sh — E97-S4 template-token placeholders + helper.
plugins/gaia/scripts/review-summary-gen.sh

# --- Bucket 2: documentation describing the dual-layout contract ---

# CLAUDE.md describes ADR-111 + the .gaia/ runtime layout (project docs).
gaia-public/CLAUDE.md

# Plugin README / CHANGELOG / ADR docs intentionally reference legacy paths
# in historical and contract sections.
plugins/gaia/CHANGELOG.md
plugins/gaia/README.md

# validator.md persona_sig docstring cites .plugin-version + legacy paths.
plugins/gaia/agents/validator.md

# SKILL.md narrative refers to legacy paths in dozens of skills — the
# guard scope was originally intended to allow narrative-only references
# while still catching executable `.sh` regressions. The blanket
# `plugins/gaia/skills/` entry made that goal a no-op (allowlisted every
# file under skills/, not just SKILL.md). AF-2026-05-21-6 tightened the
# allowlist: SKILL.md is allow-listed by glob; individual `.sh` files
# documented as legitimate-legacy smart-fallback chains are listed
# explicitly below.
#
# Matching note: the is_allowlisted helper uses `case "$rel" in "$entry"*)`,
# so glob metacharacters (`*`) are honored. `*/SKILL.md` matches any path
# whose suffix is `/SKILL.md`.
*/SKILL.md
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-create-arch/architectural-elicitation/

# Legitimate-legacy smart-fallback chains in skill scripts (canonical
# .gaia/ first, legacy second). Re-allowlisted individually per
# AF-2026-05-21-6 / Val F4.
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-dev-story/scripts/checkpoint.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-dev-story/scripts/promotion-chain-guard.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-dev-story/scripts/dod-check.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/dispatch-agent-turn.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/yield-gate.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/charter-gate.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/cite-or-flag-check.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/write-boundary.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-quick-dev/scripts/checkpoint-archive.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-quick-dev/scripts/wip-checkpoint-resolve.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-triage-findings/scripts/finalize.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-retro/scripts/finalize.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-add-feature/scripts/setup.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-add-feature/scripts/write-val-sentinel.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-deploy/scripts/deploy-dispatch.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-sprint-review/scripts/track-b-dispatch.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-sprint-review/scripts/finalize.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-sprint-close/scripts/close.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-shard-doc/scripts/h3-shard.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-shard-doc/scripts/parse-h2-boundaries.sh

# AF-2026-05-21-6 fixes — these now use ADR-111 smart-fallback (canonical
# .gaia/ first, legacy second). Allowlisted so the legacy fallback line
# below the canonical check is permitted.
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-init/scripts/generate-config.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/memory-writethrough.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-meeting/scripts/research-phase-dispatch.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-validate-prd/scripts/setup.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-create-ux/scripts/finalize.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-product-brief/scripts/finalize.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-tech-research/scripts/finalize.sh

# AF-2026-05-21-10 fix — gaia-create-prd/finalize.sh now uses the
# three-tier idiom (PRD_ARTIFACT env-var override → positive-evidence
# legacy fallback → canonical default). Allowlisted so the legacy
# `docs/planning-artifacts/prd.md` literal in the positive-evidence
# branch is permitted.
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-create-prd/scripts/finalize.sh

# AF-2026-05-21-11 fix — gaia-create-arch + gaia-edit-arch finalize.sh
# scripts now use the same three-tier idiom (ARCHITECTURE_ARTIFACT env-var
# override → positive-evidence legacy fallback → canonical default).
# Allowlisted so the legacy `docs/planning-artifacts/architecture.md`
# literal in the positive-evidence branch is permitted.
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-create-arch/scripts/finalize.sh
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-edit-arch/scripts/finalize.sh

# AF-2026-05-21-12 fix — gaia-edit-prd/setup.sh now uses the three-tier
# idiom (PRD_PATH env-var override → positive-evidence legacy fallback →
# canonical default). Allowlisted so the legacy
# `docs/planning-artifacts/prd.md` literal in the positive-evidence branch
# is permitted.
plugins/gaia/skills/gaia-edit-prd/scripts/setup.sh

# --- Bucket 3: retired scripts ---

plugins/gaia/scripts/retired/

# --- Bucket 4: test fixtures and bats files ---

plugins/gaia/tests/fixtures/
plugins/gaia/tests/

# --- Bucket 5: knowledge bases, taxonomies, rubrics ---

plugins/gaia/knowledge/
plugins/gaia/rubrics/
plugins/gaia/schemas/

# --- Bucket 6: top-level config dotfiles ---

plugins/gaia/.claude-plugin/
plugins/gaia/scripts/lib/

# --- Bucket 7: scripts whose entire purpose is the migration itself ---

plugins/gaia/scripts/migrate-stories-to-canonical-layout.sh
gaia-public/scripts/audit-v2-migration.sh

# --- Bucket 8: top-level project config schemas / hooks ---

plugins/gaia/config/
plugins/gaia/hooks/

# --- Bucket 9: agent + persona files (overlay registrations reference legacy) ---

plugins/gaia/agents/
